content:
Hi

Since it works on your tablet, for SMTP rejection check that:

Go to Settings > Account Settings > click Outgoing Server (SMTP).

Look for the entry that says yourdomain.com Click Edit.

Server Name: Change this to exactly what is on your tablet (usually mail.yourdomain.com or smtp.yourdomain.com).

Port: For POP3 accounts, this is typically 465 (with SSL/TLS) or 587 (with STARTTLS).

Username: Ensure this is your full email address.
creator: norhorn

Also the notice below the Hostname reads " Hostname is empty or invalid. Only letters,numbers, - and . are allowed " I have 2 weeks back had to do a full reinstall of Windows 11 64-bit. Had been working without any issues, then simply would not allow anything other than the full wipe out and reinstall. Both Win11 & Thunderbird are righ up to date. content:
Hi

Since the tablet works, we know the settings are correct?.

1. You use Account Wizard ? Try manually type the Server Name. If it keeps reverting delete and Add a new SMTP server entry rather than editing the broken one, then go back to your domain account's main "Server Settings" and select the new SMTP. Settings > Account Settings > Outgoing Server (SMTP) > Add.

2. Firewall or Antivirus Temporary Test: Turn off your Firewall/Antivirus and try to "Test" or "Re-run" the manual config in Thunderbird. If it works, you need to add an "Exclusion" for Thunderbird in your security software.

Ensure your outgoing port is set to 465 (SSL/TLS) or 587 (STARTTLS).

3. When you see that error Failed to find settings Verify the Authentication Method if your password is correct. Your tablet uses "Normal Password" and the PC defaults to "Encrypted Password? The "Username" field needs full email address as the username?

What is your ISP?

Because Gmail works, we know your PC can send mail. The fact that your domain fails suggests the Authentication Method or Hostname is being rejected by your ISP’s security.

account17 (imap) imap.gmail.com:993 SSL/TLS Normal password smtp.gmail.com:465 SSL/TLS Normal password

You need to set the Authentication type to OAuth2 in the Account Settings for your Gmail account. This goes for both, the incoming, and outgoing servers. Also see https://support.mozilla.org/kb/automatic-conversion-google-mail-accounts-oauth20

Wrt your themessagingco.com.au account, what error message do you get? Also see https://support.mozilla.org/kb/cannot-receive-messages

content:
Can I assume this is an IMAP account? What do you see when you view the Trash folder on your email provider's web mail interface? Are the errant messages visible there? If so, delete them there and try syncing Thunderbird again. If the messages reappear locally but not on the server, the local folder index may have become corrupt. This may fix it:

- Right-click on the folder and choose "Properties", then click the Repair button. Then compact the folder and hopefully things will show as expected. If not, let us know and there are some slightly more drastic possibilities.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/moving-thunderbird-data-to-a-new-computer

content:
This is a copy&paste from an answer I gave to a similar question a while back (see https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1570858#answer-1805771 ) ...

=

I did a little internet searching, and I don't see any sign that GMX supports OAuth2, which would explain why it's not an available option for you. I did find these generic email settings that you can try. You may need to confirm this info with their tech support:

Incoming (IMAP) Server: imap.gmx.com Port: 993 Encrytion: SSL or encryption

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 587 Encryption: STARTTLS

Outgoing (SMTP) Server: mail.gmx.com Port: 465 Encryption: SSL/TLS

Please note: You can only use a TLS protocol if your email program uses TLS versions 1.2 or 1.3. For security reasons, we no longer support the older TLS versions 1.0 and 1.1.

Info: If a connection via port 993 is not possible, you can try port 143. Please note that this connection is not encrypted by default. You can achieve encryption via port 143 by activating STARTTLS in your email program (e.g. Thunderbird, Outlook or Apple Mail) and using a valid SSL/TLS certificate.

==

I got this from:

content:
I haven't tried it yet, but seems to me that this would be effective:

Apply filter when: GETTING NEW MAIL

Match the following: Subject ISN'T: XOifdpjksdOnLKNiUfnjawe0ujncd09uu9u32n (or any nonsense string of your choice)

Perform these actions: COPY MESSAGE TO (the folder of your choice)

Worth a try?
creator: rednoise

content: P.S. - I tried it and it does work. Best to uncheck the "Run Manually" option box so you don't accidentally do that, which will copy everything in the Inbox to your chosen folder.
creator: rednoise

content:
Wow, thank you, Lin, that is so blitheringly backward, I would never have thought about it.

I just ran it manually and - viola - it copies the entire folder of already received emails.

Het new emails still don't get copied, in fact, I can't receive new emails - whaaa?!?

However, I just figured out how to do what I want:

First: 

 Left-click/select the folder I want to auto-duplicate.


Then, from the main menu: 

 Tools    Message Filters 
 Apply filter when:    Getting new mail      Filter after junk classification        O Match all messages  Perform these actions:    Copy Message To      [my new folder copy]  on local folders

.

Oy, it took a dozen iterations to test and figure out what to turn on and off.

Thanks for the trick, though - you've obviously been in places like this before, and now I'm smarter and have a new tool to try whenever I need an all-match via reverse thinking.

Thanks.
creator: peterblaise
